chief_complaint: A 75-year-old man presents with a 2-week history of worsening shortness of breath and productive cough.
history: The patient has a 10-year history of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) and has been on intermittent local corticosteroid administration. He has a history of smoking 40 packs per year. He has no history of travel or exposure to tuberculosis.
exam: Vital signs: temperature 38.5°C, pulse 110/min, blood pressure 140/90 mmHg, respiratory rate 30/min. Physical examination reveals bilateral wheezing and crackles.
labs: Chest X-ray: bilateral infiltrates. Sputum smear: acid-fast bacilli (AFB) positive. Sputum culture: Nocardia otitidiscaviarum and Mycobacterium tuberculosis. Blood tests: normal white cell count, normal C-reactive protein.
correct_diagnosis: Co-infection of Nocardia otitidiscaviarum and Mycobacterium tuberculosis
differentials: Co-infection of Nocardia otitidiscaviarum and Mycobacterium tuberculosis
correct_ranking: Co-infection of Nocardia otitidiscaviarum and Mycobacterium tuberculosis
